text: The robotic procedure begins with the surgeon opening the porta hepatis to locate the hepatic artery. Hepatic artery lymph nodes are excised and removed as specimens. The surgeon cuts connections from the common hepatic artery, proper hepatic artery, and right hepatic artery to prevent extrahepatic perfusion from the infusion pump. The gastroduodenal artery is encircled, and a branch off its right side is ligated using a suture. The common hepatic artery is fully encircled to prevent extrahepatic pump perfusion. Dissection continues up to the bifurcation of the right and left hepatic arteries, with additional dissection on the right hepatic artery. A mini Cochrane maneuver is performed on the lateral side of the porta hepatis to ensure no extrahepatic perfusion.

The surgeon clips the cystic duct and artery before removing the gallbladder from its fossa. A papaverine-soaked sponge is placed over the gastroduodenal and hepatic arteries. A pump pocket is created in the left upper quadrant, and the pump catheter is inserted through the abdominal wall into the peritoneal cavity. The gastroduodenal artery is ligated.

Bulldog clamps are placed on the common and proper hepatic artery. An 11 blade is inserted into the abdomen under direct vision to perform a precise arteriotomy on the gastrointestinal artery. A vein retractor is used to insert the pump catheter into the arteriotomy, advancing it to the junction of the common hepatic artery and gastroduodenal artery. Silk ties secure the catheter, and heparin saline is flushed through the catheter to ensure patency. Additional ties are placed around the pump and gastroduodenal artery, with checks for occlusion using catheter flushes.

Blue dye is injected, causing the liver to illuminate blue, while a small area of extrahepatic perfusion in the duodenum is corrected by ligating branches anterior to the common bile duct. Additional dissection is performed on the right side of the porta hepatis to address extrahepatic perfusion. A repeat blue dye test shows no dye filling in the duodenum. Ultrasound confirms the presence of segment three colorectal liver metastasis, and a resection margin greater than one centimeter is marked on the liver capsule.

The robotic vessel sealer is used for parenchymal dissection of the liver. The round ligament is ligated at the completion of the procedure. The video shows the hepatic artery infusion pump and the port sites used during the surgery.
